markdown.markdown:
Unnecessary re-renders are the most common performance issue in React apps. Here's how to find and fix them.
## Finding Re-renders
### React DevTools Profiler
The Profiler tab in React DevTools is the best tool for this:
1. Open React DevTools, go to the **Profiler** tab
2. Click **Record**, interact with your app, then **Stop**
3. The flame graph shows which components rendered and why
Look for components that render frequently but don't visually change — those are your targets.
### Highlight Updates
In React DevTools settings, enable **"Highlight updates when components render."** This adds a visual flash around components as they re-render. If you see the entire page flashing on every keystroke, you have a problem.
## Common Causes
### 1. New Object References
The most common cause. Every render creates new objects and arrays:
```javascript
// Bad: new object every render
<Button style={{ color: "red" }} />

// Bad: new array every render
<List items={data.filter((d) => d.active)} />
```
### 2. Inline Functions
```javascript
// Bad: new function every render
<Button onClick={() => handleClick(id)} />
```
### 3. Context Changes
When a context value changes, **every** consumer re-renders, even if they only use a part of the context that didn't change.
## Fixing Re-renders
### React.memo
Wraps a component to skip re-renders when props haven't changed:
```javascript
const ExpensiveList = React.memo(function ExpensiveList({ items }) {
  return items.map((item) => <ExpensiveItem key={item.id} item={item} />);
});
```
<callout icon="⚠️" color="yellow_bg">
	`React.memo` only does a **shallow comparison**. If you pass new object/array references as props, it won't help — you need to stabilize the references too.
</callout>
### useMemo and useCallback
```javascript
// Stabilize computed values
const filtered = useMemo(
  () => data.filter((d) => d.active),
  [data]
);

// Stabilize callbacks
const handleClick = useCallback(
  (id) => dispatch({ type: "select", id }),
  [dispatch]
);
```
### useMemo Pitfalls
- **Don't memoize everything.** Memoization has a cost (comparison + cache). Only memoize expensive computations or values passed to memoized children.
- **Missing dependencies** cause stale data. Always include everything the computation depends on.
- **useMemo doesn't guarantee** the value persists — React may evict it. Don't use it for side effects.
---
The approach: **measure first** with the Profiler, identify the biggest offenders, then apply targeted fixes. Resist the urge to wrap everything in `React.memo` — most components don't need it.

rich_text.plain_text: How to find and fix unnecessary re-renders in React using DevTools Profiler, React.memo, and common useMemo pitfalls.
